text: The FDA has issued an urgent recall for frozen spinach products manufactured by GreenLeaf Foods due to potential Listeria monocytogenes contamination. Testing revealed the presence of the pathogen in multiple production lots. Consumers are advised to discard any affected products immediately. The risk to immunocompromised individuals is classified as high.

A batch of organic peanut butter from NutriSpread Inc. was found to contain undeclared milk proteins. This poses a serious risk to individuals with dairy allergies. The company has initiated a voluntary recall of all 16-oz jars with best-by dates between March and June 2025.

Several consumers reported finding small metal fragments in canned tomato soup produced by HomeTaste Corp. An investigation traced the contamination to a faulty processing machine. The risk of injury from ingestion is moderate, and a product recall has been announced for lot numbers HT-2024-089 through HT-2024-112.

Elevated levels of lead were detected in imported turmeric powder sold under the SpiceWorld brand. Laboratory analysis showed concentrations exceeding the permissible limit by a factor of three. Long-term consumption may pose chronic health risks. Health authorities have issued an advisory to stop using the product.

A routine inspection at FreshCatch Seafood revealed that refrigerated shrimp products had been stored above safe temperature thresholds for an extended period. Salmonella contamination was confirmed in follow-up testing. The affected products have been removed from store shelves, and a Class I recall is in effect.

BakeryDelights reported that a limited run of their wheat-free cookies may contain traces of wheat flour due to a production line error. While the concentration is low, this may affect individuals with celiac disease or wheat sensitivity. The company issued a precautionary warning for products sold in the northeastern region.

Imported dried chili peppers from two suppliers were found to be contaminated with aflatoxins at levels exceeding regulatory limits. Aflatoxin B1, a potent carcinogen, was detected at 28 ppb compared to the 10 ppb maximum. The affected shipments have been detained at customs and will not enter the consumer market.

A consumer complaint led to the discovery of glass shards in bottled apple juice from OrchardFresh. The contamination was traced to a breakage incident on the bottling line. Three injuries have been reported. An immediate recall of all 32-oz bottles produced on November 15 has been issued with a high urgency classification.

Routine monitoring detected low levels of E. coli O157:H7 in pre-washed salad mix distributed by Valley Greens. The contamination appears linked to irrigation water at one supplier farm. Although no illnesses have been reported, the products have been pulled as a precautionary measure and a warning has been issued to retailers.

FarmFresh Dairy announced that their vanilla-flavored yogurt cups may contain sulfites not listed on the ingredient label. Sulfites can trigger severe reactions in sensitive individuals, particularly those with asthma. The company is working with regulators to update labeling and has issued an allergy alert for products with a shelf life through April 2025.
